description: BIS Standard for Aerosol Deodorants and Body Sprays. Includes limits for propellants impurities, heavy metals, and antimicrobial agents.

rules.name: Benzene Ban
rules.description: Benzene is a known human carcinogen and is strictly banned as an impurity in aerosol propellants.

rules.name: Methanol Limit
rules.description: Methanol (methyl alcohol) must not exceed 0.2% as an impurity in the denatured ethanol base to prevent systemic toxicity.

rules.name: Triclosan Antimicrobial Limit
rules.description: Triclosan used as an antibacterial agent in deodorants must not exceed 0.3%.

rules.name: Lead Limit
rules.description: Lead (Pb) impurity must not exceed 20 ppm in cosmetic sprays.

rules.name: Arsenic Limit
rules.description: Arsenic (As) impurity must not exceed 2 ppm.

rules.name: Heavy Metals Cumulative Limit
rules.description: Cumulative sum of Lead + Arsenic must not exceed 22 ppm.

rules.name: Aluminum Chlorohydrate Limit
rules.description: Aluminum Chlorohydrate (active antiperspirant ingredient) is typically limited to 25% in aerosol formulations.

rules.name: Ethanol Content Limit
rules.description: Ethyl alcohol (denatured) in body sprays and perfumes should not exceed 60% for safe aerosol dispensing and flammability control.

rules.name: Butane/Propellant Limit
rules.description: Butane/Isobutane propellant gas should not exceed 40% of the aerosol formulation to maintain safe internal pressure.

rules.name: Total Fragrance Limit
rules.description: Total fragrance compounds (including Linalool and Limonene) should not exceed 5% in deodorant/body spray formulations.
